justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named and described as 'mutually exclusive as specified'. (2) All three agents' positions correctly attributed — Bird (100ms consistency), MJ (30s CDN/Fastly for scalability), Pippen (confirmed CDN required for 50k load and that staleness violates Bird's rule). (3) Escalation produced with 4 concrete, distinct resolution paths (relax Bird's SLA, remove CDN from read path, two-tier read model, selectively scope 100ms guarantee). (4) No implementation brief produced — explicitly states 'Shaq cannot implement both simultaneously' and defers to domain/architecture decision. (5) Resolution paths are concrete and distinct.

observations.text: Escalation description is precise and well-structured, clearly stating why the constraints are incompatible
Four resolution paths provided (exceeds the 3 minimum), each with clear trade-offs explained
Correctly attributes Pippen's role as confirming the conflict rather than just raising an operational concern
Explicitly states Shaq cannot implement both simultaneously — no ambiguous brief produced
Low confidence level (30) is appropriate given the unresolved contradiction requiring human decision
Assumptions section clearly documents what Magic is taking as given, making the analysis auditable
Recommendation correctly identifies this as requiring a domain or architecture decision above Shaq's authority

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named and described in detail. All three agents' positions correctly attributed — Bird (100ms rule), MJ (30s CDN for scalability), Pippen (independent confirmation of incompatibility). observations.text: Conflict described with exceptional clarity, including the 300x factor between 100ms and 30s
Four resolution paths provided, all concrete and distinct: relax Bird's rule, remove CDN, tiered freshness, push-based delivery
Recommendation section adds nuance by questioning whether Bird's rule applies to all viewers or just the scoring player
Low confidence level (30) is well-calibrated — correctly identifies the ambiguity as load-bearing
Pippen's finding treated as a conflict indicator, not just an operational concern
Assumptions section transparently documents interpretation choices that could affect the resolution
